input: Under our Terms of Service, we collect your personal data including name, Hong Kong identity card number, residential address, income information, and transaction history. This data is used to verify your identity, maintain your account, and comply with legal obligations under the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance. We may transfer your personal data to our group companies in mainland China for back-office processing. You consent to this transfer by using our services. Data transferred to the mainland will be subject to the laws of the People's Republic of China.

Before investing in this structured product, you should be aware that the product's performance is linked to the performance of a basket of underlying shares. In the worst case, you may receive shares of the worst-performing stock in the basket at an effective purchase price that is higher than the market price at maturity. This is not a principal-protected product. The maximum potential loss is the full amount invested if all reference stocks fall to zero. Past performance of the basket components is not indicative of future results.

Our Credit Risk Management Policy requires us to maintain a diversified loan portfolio with no single counterparty exposure exceeding 25% of our capital base, in accordance with the HKMA's large exposure limits under the Banking Ordinance. Exposures approaching the limit trigger a mandatory credit committee review. All connected party transactions are subject to enhanced approval requirements including independent valuation, board-level sign-off, and disclosure in our annual report as required under the Banking Ordinance section on connected party transactions.

This is to confirm that we have conducted our annual review of your investment portfolio in accordance with our ongoing client review obligations. The review assessed whether your portfolio remains suitable given any changes in your personal circumstances, financial situation, and investment objectives since our last review. You confirmed that your circumstances are unchanged. Based on this assessment, we are satisfied that the current asset allocation remains consistent with your documented risk profile. A record of this review has been retained in your client file.

As an SFC-licensed Money Market Fund, we are required to maintain a weighted average maturity of no more than 60 days and a weighted average life of no more than 120 days for our portfolio. Our current weighted average maturity is 42 days and our weighted average life is 87 days, both within regulatory limits. We invest exclusively in HKD-denominated instruments rated at least A-1 by Standard and Poor's or equivalent. Our NAV is calculated daily and published on our website and the SFC's fund database.

Our Complaint Handling Policy requires that all written complaints be acknowledged within five business days of receipt. Complex complaints may require up to 45 business days for full resolution. Where resolution exceeds 30 business days, a written status update must be sent to the complainant explaining the reason for delay and the expected resolution date. All complaints are logged in our centralised Compliance Register and reviewed quarterly by the Chief Compliance Officer. Patterns of recurring complaints are escalated to the Board Risk Committee.

We wish to confirm that our firm's anti-market misconduct training programme, completed by all licensed staff annually, covers the statutory definitions of market misconduct under Part XIII of the Securities and Futures Ordinance, including insider dealing, false trading, price rigging, disclosure of information about prohibited transactions, and stock market manipulation. Licensed staff must achieve a minimum score of 80% in the post-training assessment. Failure to complete training or achieve the minimum score is escalated to the Responsible Officer and recorded in the staff compliance record.

We are approaching you today to offer an opportunity to invest in our new Quantum Yield Note, which is offered exclusively through private placement to accredited investors. This instrument provides quarterly distributions linked to a proprietary algorithmic strategy. We want to be transparent: neither the strategy documentation, the offering memorandum, nor the issuer's financial statements have been independently audited or verified by an external party. The issuer is a newly incorporated Cayman Islands entity with no operating history. Minimum investment is HK$500,000.

I placed a series of large buy orders in Goldfield Mining shares at the open today and then cancelled them before execution, creating the appearance of strong buying interest. This caused several retail investors to purchase shares at elevated prices. I then sold my pre-existing position at the inflated price into the buying interest I had created. Once my sell was complete, I cancelled all outstanding buy orders and the stock price immediately fell back to its previous level.

Our financial planning chatbot has been generating income projections for retirement planning purposes. The chatbot's outputs include statements such as 'at your current savings rate, you are on track for a comfortable retirement' and 'based on historical average returns, your portfolio should reach HK$3.2 million by age 65.' These statements are generated without a suitability assessment, are not reviewed by a licensed professional before delivery, and are distributed to all users regardless of their financial profile.
